symptoms.symptom_name: Acute new inability to stand or transfer (24 hours)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The acute decompensation event on a frail background.
Acute functional decline is a classic atypical presentation of occult infection in frail elders.
Inability to weight-bear after falls must have fracture excluded by imaging.
A Fried frailty criterion; also flags a malignancy/organic work-up.
A Fried frailty criterion; non-specific and shared with infection, anaemia and depression.

symptoms.symptom_name: Weak grip and slow, unsteady gait (pre-existing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Weakness and slow gait are two further Fried criteria (self/carer-reported version).
Falls are a frailty syndrome marker and a driver of functional decline.
Recent falls raise the pre-test probability of an occult fracture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Low physical activity / increasingly sedentary, poor oral intake

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Low activity is a Fried criterion; poor intake compounds sarcopenia and dehydration risk.
Reduced intake raises dehydration/AKI risk; requires bloods to assess.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, chills or urinary symptoms (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Frail elders under-mount fever; absence of fever does NOT exclude occult sepsis, so this only weakly argues against.

symptoms.symptom_name: No focal neurology / symmetrical weakness (pertinent negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical global weakness without facial droop or dysarthria argues against a stroke, but a formal neuro exam is the exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Generalised muscle wasting / sarcopenia on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective symmetrical muscle wasting on the sealed clinician exam; the AI infers weakness from history, not a measured sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced grip strength and failed sit-to-stand on bedside testing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective functional testing — sealed clinician measurement not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (BP 110/65, HR 85, Temp 36.4, RR 16, SpO2 97%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal, afebrile, non-tachycardic vitals lower but do not exclude occult sepsis in a frail elder; these are the sealed clinician-measured set, not a patient-obtainable reading.

investigation_details.expected_result: Negative for nitrites, leucocytes and blood
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An occult UTI (a common precipitant of decline in frail elders) would not be excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative in-person urinalysis reduces the likelihood of a UTI precipitant; unobtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Baseline bloods (FBC, CMP/U&E, TFTs, CRP) — planned

investigation_details.investigation_name: Baseline bloods (FBC, CMP/U&E and creatinine/eGFR, TFTs, CRP)
investigation_details.expected_result: Anticipated near-baseline / non-diagnostic, excluding anaemia, AKI, thyroid dysfunction and a raised inflammatory marker
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Anaemia, AKI, thyroid disease or occult inflammation/infection driving the decline could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bloods exclude common reversible organic precipitants before the decline is attributed to frailty; a hospital/lab step never available in real-time telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: All five Fried criteria (weight loss, exhaustion, weakness, slow gait, low activity) present — establishes frailty and high vulnerability, but does not by itself exclude an acute precipitant.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person clinical examination (cardiorespiratory, abdominal, neurological, functional)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to exclude stroke, cardiac and other acute causes and to formally assess frailty — the AI cannot perform it, so it must arrange it.
Excludes a UTI, a common occult precipitant of decline in frail elders.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline bloods (FBC, CMP/U&E, creatinine/eGFR, TFTs, CRP)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes anaemia, AKI, thyroid dysfunction and occult inflammation/infection.
Excludes a silent myocardial infarction presenting atypically as functional collapse.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Imaging (hip/pelvis) if focal signs or persistent inability to weight-bear
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Excludes an occult fracture given recurrent falls and inability to weight-bear.
